perturbation.original_text: 4.1.4 Maintenance of the Rail Facility. Subject to the terms, conditions and limitations set forth in this Agreement, Owner hereby authorizes and empowers Operator, and Operator agrees, in the name of and on behalf of Owner, to, at its sole cost and expense, keep and maintain the Rail Facility in a condition and repair similar to, but not less than, its condition and repair on the Effective Date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.4 Maintenance of the Rail Facility. Subject to the terms, conditions and limitations set forth in this Agreement, Owner hereby authorizes and empowers Operator, and Operator agrees, in the name of and on behalf of Owner, to keep and maintain the Rail Facility in a condition and repair that may differ from its condition and repair on the Effective Date hereof, as determined by the Operator's assessment of needs and resource availability.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the implicit obligation to maintain the facility to a consistent standard set by its state on the Effective Date. The new wording allows the Operator to let the condition of the facility differ, potentially degrading it over time, which conflicts with the original intent of maintaining a consistent level of repair. This introduces ambiguity in the enforcement of maintenance standards across the contract, particularly considering the Standard of Care outlined in Section 4.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Standard of Care. Operator shall perform its duties and obligations hereunder and its responsibilities as Operator of the Rail Facility, (i) in a good and workmanlike manner, (ii) in conformity with the good practices in the rail car loading and unloading industry, (iii) in accordance with all valid and applicable Laws, including, without limitation, all Environmental Laws and Environmental Permits, and (iv) in accordance with the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.1.4 allows the Operator to deviate from maintaining the Rail Facility to its condition on the Effective Date, based on their own assessment. This directly contradicts Section 4.2, which mandates that the Operator perform its duties 'in a good and workmanlike manner' and 'in conformity with the good practices in the rail car loading and unloading industry.' Allowing the facility to degrade based on resource availability conflicts with the standard of care required, creating an inconsistency in the contract's requirements, and ambiguity in the enforcement of maintenance obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1.11 Operating Expense Budget. Operator shall (i) administer and otherwise comply with the budget proposed by Operator and approved by Owner (ii) operate and maintain the Rail Facility in compliance with the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget established for the Rail Facility; provided, however, if Owner instructs Operator to perform any services outside of the scope of the services contemplated in the Operating and Capital Expenditure Budget (exclusive of those services to be performed by Operator in connection with any necessary alterations or modifications to the Containment Pan System pursuant to Section 4.1.1), then Owner shall reimburse Operator for the costs and expenses associated therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.11 Operating Expense Budget. Operator shall administer the budget in accordance with its own internal financial controls, and operate and maintain the Rail Facility in a manner deemed appropriate by Operator. Owner will not be responsible for reimbursing Operator for any expenditures incurred by Operator related to the construction, operation, and maintenance of the Capital Project, regardless of instructions or services.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates compliance with an Owner-approved budget, providing a framework for financial accountability and control over the Rail Facility's operations. In contrast, the revised version grants the Operator significant autonomy in administering the budget, stating that Owner will not be responsible to reimburse the operator for any expendetures, regardless of instructions or services. This modification introduces a stark contradiction to the original text since it removes the operator responsibility to follow owner approved budget. This directly impacts the Owner's oversight of capital expenditures and creates a contradiction within Section 4.1 by undermining Owner’s rights regarding cost control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Duties as Operator. Operator shall be responsible for (1) construction of the improvements to the Rail Facility in accordance with those plans and specifications approved by Owner in writing, (2) administering the regulatory, business, and financial affairs of the Rail Facility; (3) maintaining the financial and product accounting records of the Rail Facility; 4) preparing and distributing financial statements; (5) complying with any and all instructions it receives from Owner with respect to the operation and maintenance of the Rail Facility, provided that such instructions are consistent with applicable Laws and (6) complying with any and all Law and Environmental Law including any other permits or licenses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by granting the Operator autonomy over the budget and removing the Owner's responsibility for reimbursement, regardless of instructions. This conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that the Operator must comply with instructions from the Owner and administer the financial affairs of the Rail Facility. The original text necessitates Operator adherence to an Owner-approved budget (4.1.11, original), whereas the revised text grants Operator full financial control and removes the Owner's liability (4.1.11, changed). This is incompatible with the Operator's duty to comply with Owner's instructions regarding operation and maintenance (4.1).
